message.content:
Repo: /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay (branch harden/pass-1). Venv (run PoCs): /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python.
This is a dataset-agnostic ML/methodology engine being hardened for PRODUCTION. Hard constraints (do NOT flag as bugs): local-first/data-sovereign (loopback only, nothing leaves the box), no metered API (subscription OAuth only), engine never imports adapters/datasets (ADR-0002), optional heavy backends are lazy-imported (ADR-0006). Read real code; prefer findings you can back with a concrete repro/PoC against the venv. Report ONLY genuine production-readiness defects (not style/nits). Each finding needs a real file path and, where possible, a line.

You are independent VERIFIER #1 of 2. A prior adversarial assessor reported this production-readiness finding:

TITLE: ProvenanceTrail.record performs a non-atomic check-then-act on shared list state (not thread-safe)
DIMENSION: correctness & concurrency
CLAIMED SEVERITY: LOW
FILE: src/assay_engine/provenance.py 112-150
DESCRIPTION: record() computes `seq = len(self._entries)` and `prev_hash = self._entries[-1].entry_hash` (read), then does work (clock, digest, freeze), then `self._entries.append(entry)` (write), with no lock. If a single ProvenanceTrail were shared across threads, two concurrent record() calls could read the same seq/prev_hash and append two entries that both claim e.g. seq=N with the same prev_hash, breaking the chain so verify_records() (which asserts e.seq == i and e.prev_hash == prev) would fail — a corrupted append-only audit trail, the exact 'no step can be removed or reordered' guarantee the trail exists to provide.

Current exposure is latent, not live: run_study (pipeline.py) drives the trail strictly single-threaded, and as_recorder() — the only path that hands a trail-bound recorder to external code — is defined but not wired anywhere in the engine, and the reasoning ThreadPoolExecutor does not touch the trail. The docstring also scopes the trail to 'one study run.' Severity is LOW because no concurrent caller exists today, but the class offers a public record()/as_recorder() API with no documented single-thread constraint, so a future concurrent gate/recorder wiring would silently corrupt provenance.
SUGGESTED FIX: Guard the read-compute-append sequence with a threading.Lock held across the seq/prev_hash read through the append (or document the trail as single-thread-only and assert it). Given record() also calls an injected clock and a hashing step, hold the lock for the whole method to keep seq/prev_hash/entry_hash mutually consistent.

Independently determine whether this is a TRUE production-readiness problem. Read the actual code; attempt a concrete PoC against the venv if applicable. Set is_real=false if it is a false positive, already-handled, or merely a documented/justified design choice given the hard constraints. Recalibrate severity. Provide your evidence (what you read/ran).

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.content:
1	"""Append-only provenance trail (GOVERNANCE §3, W3C PROV-DM style).
2	
3	Every action a study takes is recorded, in order, before the next executes: ingestion, the
4	blind baseline build, each discovered/locked hypothesis, every gate decision, each verdict,
5	the source scorecard, the final report. The trail is the spine of reproducibility and of the
6	independence claim — "no step can be retroactively removed or reordered" (GOVERNANCE §3).
7	
8	The trail enforces that structurally, not by promise:
9	
10	- **Append-only.** Entries are added through :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.record` only; there is no
11	  remove/edit/reorder API and the exposed view is an immutable tuple of frozen entries.
12	- **Chained.** Each entry carries ``prev_hash`` and an ``entry_hash`` over
13	  ``(prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp)`` using the engine's one type-faithful
14	  serializer (:mod:`assay_engine._canonical`), rooted at a fixed genesis.
15	
16	Integrity — honest scope, two tiers:
17	
18	- **Unkeyed (default).** The chain is plain SHA-256. It is *tamper-evident against naive
19	  tampering and accidental corruption*: editing one entry without recomputing downstream
20	  hashes, reordering, or deleting an entry is caught by :func:`verify_records`. It is **not**
21	  forgery-proof: a motivated party who controls the serialized bytes can edit a payload and
22	  recompute the whole genesis-rooted chain, and the result verifies. Unkeyed integrity buys
23	  "is this a valid chain?", not "is this *the* chain the engine produced."
24	- **Keyed (``secret=...``).** Pass an on-box secret (as pre-registration does, ADR-0009) and
25	  the chain is HMAC-SHA256. The head cannot be recomputed without the secret, so a downstream
26	  store cannot silently rewrite history and re-seal it; :func:`verify_records` must be given
27	  the same secret. This is local tamper-*resistance*; non-repudiable third-party attestation of
28	  the trail's time remains the same pluggable concern as pre-registration, out of scope here.
29	"""
30	
31	from __future__ import annotations
32	
33	import datetime as _dt
34	import hashlib
35	import hmac
36	from dataclasses import dataclass
37	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping
38	
39	from assay_engine._canonical import canonical_json
40	from assay_engine._frozen import freeze_mapping
41	
42	_UTC = _dt.timezone.utc
43	_GENESIS = "assay-provenance:v1:genesis"
44	
45	Clock = Callable[[], _dt.datetime]
46	
47	
48	class ProvenanceError(RuntimeError):
49	    """The provenance trail is inconsistent (a broken chain) or an entry cannot be recorded."""
50	
51	
52	@dataclass(frozen=True, slots=True)
53	class ProvenanceEntry:
54	    """One immutable, chained record of a single action."""
55	
56	    seq: int
57	    kind: str
58	    summary: str
59	    payload: Mapping[str, Any]
60	    timestamp: str  # ISO-8601 UTC
61	    prev_hash: str
62	    entry_hash: str
63	
64	    def __post_init__(self) -> None:
65	        object.__setattr__(self, "payload", freeze_mapping(self.payload))
66	
67	
68	def _digest(
69	    seq: int,
70	    kind: str,
71	    summary: str,
72	    payload: Mapping[str, Any],
73	    timestamp: str,
74	    prev_hash: str,
75	    secret: bytes | None,
76	) -> str:
77	    try:
78	        msg = canonical_json(
79	            ["assay-prov:v1", prev_hash, seq, kind, summary, dict(payload), timestamp]
80	        ).encode("utf-8")
81	    except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
82	        # RecursionError (deeply nested payload) is a RuntimeError, not Value/TypeError — catch it
83	        # too so the entrypoints surface a typed ProvenanceError, never a raw exception (#89).
84	        raise ProvenanceError(
85	            f"provenance payload for {kind!r} cannot be serialized reproducibly ({exc}); "
86	            "record only JSON-native / hashable values of bounded depth"
87	        ) from None
88	    if secret is None:
89	        return hashlib.sha256(msg).hexdigest()
90	    return hmac.new(secret, msg, "sha256").hexdigest()
91	
92	
93	def _utc_now() -> _dt.datetime:
94	    return _dt.datetime.now(tz=_UTC)
95	
96	
97	class ProvenanceTrail:
98	    """An append-only, chained audit trail for one study run.
99	
100	    ``secret`` (>= 16 bytes) keys the chain with HMAC so a serialized trail cannot be forged
101	    without it (see module docstring). ``clock`` injects the timestamp source (deterministic
102	    tests); it must return a timezone-aware ``datetime``.
103	    """
104	
105	    def __init__(self, *, secret: bytes | None = None, clock: Clock | None = None) -> None:
106	        if secret is not None and (not isinstance(secret, (bytes, bytearray)) or len(secret) < 16):
107	            raise ValueError("provenance secret must be >= 16 bytes of key material")
108	        self._entries: list[ProvenanceEntry] = []
109	        self._secret: bytes | None = bytes(secret) if secret is not None else None
110	        self._clock: Clock = clock or _utc_now
111	
112	    def record(self, kind: str, summary: str, **payload: Any) -> ProvenanceEntry:
113	        """Append one entry. Returns it. The only way to add to the trail."""
114	        if not kind:
115	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance entry kind must be non-empty")
116	        seq = len(self._entries)
117	        prev_hash = self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
118	        try:
119	            instant = self._clock()
120	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the clock is an injected dependency
121	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance clock raised: {exc}") from None
122	        if not isinstance(instant, _dt.datetime):
123	            raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a datetime")
124	        try:
125	            # utcoffset()/astimezone()/isoformat() can raise on a hostile datetime subclass or an
126	            # out-of-range value — keep the typed-error contract (#96).
127	            if instant.tzinfo is None or instant.utcoffset() is None:
128	                raise ProvenanceError("provenance clock must return a timezone-aware datetime")
129	            timestamp = instant.astimezone(_UTC).isoformat()
130	        except ProvenanceError:
131	            raise
132	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001
133	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance timestamp could not be normalized: {exc}") from None
134	        entry_hash = _digest(seq, kind, summary, payload, timestamp, prev_hash, self._secret)
135	        try:
136	            entry = ProvenanceEntry(
137	                seq=seq,
138	                kind=kind,
139	                summary=summary,
140	                payload=payload,
141	                timestamp=timestamp,
142	                prev_hash=prev_hash,
143	                entry_hash=entry_hash,
144	            )
145	        except (ValueError, TypeError, RecursionError) as exc:
146	            raise ProvenanceError(
147	                f"provenance entry for {kind!r} could not be frozen ({exc})"
148	            ) from None
149	        self._entries.append(entry)
150	        return entry
151	
152	    def as_recorder(self) -> Callable[[Any], None]:
153	        """A :data:`gates.ProvenanceRecorder` bound to this trail (records GateDecisions).
154	
155	        Reads each attribute exactly once and defensively, so a booby-trapped decision object
156	        (a property that raises) surfaces a typed ``ProvenanceError`` rather than a raw error.
157	        """
158	
159	        def _record(decision: Any) -> None:
160	            try:
161	                gate = getattr(decision, "gate", None)
162	                approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
163	                reason = getattr(decision, "reason", "")
164	                evidence = dict(getattr(decision, "evidence", {}) or {})
165	            except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — the decision is on the trust boundary
166	                raise ProvenanceError(
167	                    f"could not read gate decision for provenance: {exc}"
168	                ) from None
169	            self.record(
170	                "gate",
171	                f"gate {gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
172	                gate=gate,
173	                approved=approved,
174	                reason=reason,
175	                evidence=evidence,
176	            )
177	
178	        return _record
179	
180	    @property
181	    def entries(self) -> tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]:
182	        return tuple(self._entries)
183	
184	    @property
185	    def head(self) -> str:
186	        """The current chain head (last entry_hash, or the genesis if empty)."""
187	        return self._entries[-1].entry_hash if self._entries else _GENESIS
188	
189	    def __len__(self) -> int:
190	        return len(self._entries)
191	
192	    def verify(self) -> None:
193	        """Recompute the chain; raise :class:`ProvenanceError` on any inconsistency."""
194	        verify_records(self._entries, secret=self._secret)
195	
196	    def to_records(self) -> tuple[dict[str, Any], ...]:
197	        """Serialize to plain dicts (for a persistent store); re-checkable via :func:`verify_records`."""
198	        return tuple(
199	            {
200	                "seq": e.seq,
201	                "kind": e.kind,
202	                "summary": e.summary,
203	                "payload": dict(e.payload),
204	                "timestamp": e.timestamp,
205	                "prev_hash": e.prev_hash,
206	                "entry_hash": e.entry_hash,
207	            }
208	            for e in self._entries
209	        )
210	
211	
212	def verify_records(
213	    entries: "list[ProvenanceEntry] | tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]",
214	    *,
215	    secret: bytes | None = None,
216	) -> None:
217	    """Verify a sequence of entries forms an intact chain (order, linkage, content).
218	
219	    Pass the same ``secret`` the trail was keyed with; an unkeyed trail verifies with
220	    ``secret=None``. A keyed trail will not verify under the wrong/absent secret.
221	    """
222	    prev = _GENESIS
223	    for i, e in enumerate(entries):
224	        if e.seq != i:
225	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} has seq {e.seq} (reordered or removed)")
226	        if e.prev_hash != prev:
227	            raise ProvenanceError(f"provenance entry {i} prev_hash breaks the chain")
228	        recomputed = _digest(e.seq, e.kind, e.summary, e.payload, e.timestamp, e.prev_hash, secret)
229	        if not hmac.compare_digest(recomputed, e.entry_hash):
230	            raise ProvenanceError(
231	                f"provenance entry {i} content was altered, or the wrong key was used (hash mismatch)"
232	            )
233	        prev = e.entry_hash
234	
235	
236	def from_records(
237	    records: "list[Mapping[str, Any]] | tuple[Mapping[str, Any], ...]",
238	    *,
239	    secret: bytes | None = None,
240	) -> tuple[ProvenanceEntry, ...]:
241	    """Rebuild entries from :meth:`ProvenanceTrail.to_records` output and verify the chain."""
242	    entries = tuple(
243	        ProvenanceEntry(
244	            seq=r["seq"],
245	            kind=r["kind"],
246	            summary=r["summary"],
247	            payload=r["payload"],
248	            timestamp=r["timestamp"],
249	            prev_hash=r["prev_hash"],
250	            entry_hash=r["entry_hash"],
251	        )
252	        for r in records
253	    )
254	    verify_records(entries, secret=secret)
255	    return entries
256

content.text: The code matches the finding's description. The check-then-act on `self._entries` (lines 116-117 read, line 149 append) is indeed not guarded by a lock. Now let me verify the exposure claim: is the trail ever used concurrently?

content.text: Let me check the reasoning seam ThreadPoolExecutor and whether the pool-executed function touches the trail.

content.content:
130	
131	
132	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
133	# Bounded timeout pool with saturation guard
134	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
135	_POOL_WORKERS = 8
136	_pool = ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=_POOL_WORKERS, thread_name_prefix="reasoning-timeout")
137	_inflight = 0
138	_inflight_lock = threading.Lock()
139	
140	
141	def _release(_future: Any) -> None:
142	    global _inflight
143	    with _inflight_lock:
144	        _inflight -= 1
145	
146	
147	def _submit_bounded(fn: Callable[[], Any]) -> Any:
148	    """Submit to the shared pool, refusing if every worker slot is already in flight
149	    (hung backend calls would otherwise leak all slots)."""
150	    global _inflight
151	    with _inflight_lock:
152	        if _inflight >= _POOL_WORKERS:
153	            raise PermanentReasoningError(
154	                f"reasoning timeout pool saturated ({_inflight}/{_POOL_WORKERS} in-flight) — "
155	                "hung backend call(s) have leaked every worker slot"
156	            )
157	        _inflight += 1
158	    try:
159	        future = _pool.submit(fn)
160	    except BaseException:
161	        with _inflight_lock:
162	            _inflight -= 1
163	        raise
164	    future.add_done_callback(_release)
165	    return future
166	
167	
168	def _with_timeout(fn: Callable[[], Any], timeout: float, what: str) -> Any:
169	    """Run a sync callable with a hard wall-clock timeout via the bounded pool."""
170	    future = _submit_bounded(fn)
171	    try:
172	        return future.result(timeout=timeout)
173	    except FuturesTimeout as exc:
174	        # No future.cancel(): a ThreadPoolExecutor cannot interrupt a thread already blocked
175	        # in a backend call. The worker keeps its slot until the call returns; the bounded
176	        # pool's saturation guard is the real protection against leaked/hung slots.
177	        raise ReasoningError(f"{what} timed out after {timeout:.0f}s") from exc
178	
179	
180	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
181	# JSON extraction — balanced-brace walker (robust to fences / trailing prose)
182	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
183	def _first_balanced_json(text: str) -> str | None:
184	    """Return the first balanced ``{...}`` or ``[...]`` span, respecting string literals.
185	
186	    A greedy regex grabs from the first brace to the LAST brace, mangling replies with
187	    multiple JSON values or braces in trailing prose; this walks the structure honestly.
188	    """
189	    start = None

content.text: Confirmed: the reasoning ThreadPoolExecutor runs backend-call functions only; it does not touch the trail. Now let me check how the trail is used in pipeline.py — is run_study single-threaded, and is `as_recorder` wired into gates anywhere?

301	                        f"(e.g. {sorted(unknown)[:3]}) — partition would silently drop units"
302	                    )
303	            # optional dataset features (recorded + returned; the baseline builder is blind-built
304	            # from the corpus, so features are provenance/output, not a baseline input here)
305	            feature_matrices: list[FeatureMatrix] = []
306	            corpus_unit_ids = {u.unit_id for u in corpus.units}
307	            for fb in defn.feature_builders:
308	                fm = fb.build(corpus)
309	                # features must describe THIS corpus's units — no fictional or duplicate ids
310	                # (the feature-side analogue of the split-drift guard above).
311	                if len(set(fm.unit_ids)) != len(fm.unit_ids):
312	                    raise FirewallViolation(
313	                        f"feature builder {list(fb.provides)} returned duplicate unit_ids"
314	                    )
315	                ghost = set(fm.unit_ids) - corpus_unit_ids
316	                if ghost:
317	                    raise FirewallViolation(
318	                        f"feature builder {list(fb.provides)} returned {len(ghost)} unit_id(s) "
319	                        f"absent from the corpus (e.g. {sorted(ghost)[:3]})"
320	                    )
321	                feature_matrices.append(fm)
322	                trail.record(
323	                    "features",
324	                    f"built feature matrix ({len(fm.feature_names)} features)",
325	                    provides=list(fb.provides),
326	                    n_units=len(fm.unit_ids),
327	                    feature_names=list(fm.feature_names),
328	                )
329	
330	        # ---- BASELINE (blind — Firewall A). Capture the ordering instant BEFORE the build. ----
331	        baseline_instant = _now()
332	        with tracer.span("phase:BASELINE"):
333	            enter(Phase.BASELINE)
334	            guard: ClaimBlindGuard[Any] = ClaimBlindGuard()
335	            with guard.sealed():
336	                baseline = plan.baseline_builder.build(corpus, claim_guard=guard)
337	            if baseline.corpus_fingerprint != cfp:
338	                raise FirewallViolation(
339	                    "baseline corpus_fingerprint does not match the ingested corpus — the baseline "
340	                    "was not built from this corpus"
341	                )
342	            trail.record(
343	                "baseline",
344	                "built blind baseline (Firewall A)",
345	                corpus_fingerprint=baseline.corpus_fingerprint,
346	                contents=sorted(baseline.contents),
347	                determinism=dict(baseline.determinism),
348	            )
349	
350	        discovery_verdicts: tuple[Verdict, ...] = ()
351	        scorecard: SourceScorecard | None = None
352	
353	        # ---- DISCOVERY → PREREGISTER → gate → CONFIRM (Firewall B) ----
354	        if StudyMode.DISCOVERY in modes:
355	            assert plan.split is not None and plan.discover is not None
356	            assert plan.confirm_held_out is not None
357	            discovery_corpus = subset_corpus(corpus, plan.split.discovery_ids)
358	            held_out = subset_corpus(corpus, plan.split.confirm_ids)
359	
360	            with tracer.span("phase:DISCOVERY"):
361	                enter(Phase.DISCOVERY)
362	                if not discovery_corpus.units:
363	                    raise FirewallViolation("discovery partition selects no corpus units")
364	                hypotheses = list(plan.discover(discovery_corpus))  # only the discovery partition
365	                trail.record(
366	                    "discovery",
367	                    f"data surfaced {len(hypotheses)} candidate hypotheses",
368	                    n=len(hypotheses),
369	                    ids=[h.hypothesis_id for h in hypotheses],
370	                )
371	
372	            with tracer.span("phase:PREREGISTER"):
373	                enter(Phase.PREREGISTER)
374	                if not held_out.units:
375	                    raise FirewallViolation("confirmation partition selects no corpus units")
376	                preregister_instant = _now()
377	                for h in hypotheses:
378	                    if h.origin is not HypothesisOrigin.DISCOVERY:
379	                        raise FirewallViolation(
380	                            f"hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r} is {h.origin.value!r}; the discovery "
381	                            "phase expects DISCOVERY-origin (data-surfaced) hypotheses"
382	                        )
383	                    vt = require_preregistered(
384	                        h, authority=plan.authority, not_after=preregister_instant
385	                    )
386	                    trail.record(
387	                        "preregister",
388	                        f"locked hypothesis {h.hypothesis_id!r}",
389	                        hypothesis_id=h.hypothesis_id,

content.text: The pipeline is strictly sequential (`trail.record` calls happen inline within phase spans, one after another). Let me confirm the gates `record` callback and whether anything wires `as_recorder` into gate execution, and that `run_study` itself isn't invoked concurrently per-trail.

content.content:
60	    """
61	
62	    name: str
63	    frm: Phase
64	    to: Phase
65	    precondition: Callable[[Mapping[str, Any]], GateDecision]
66	    requires_human: bool = False
67	    modes: frozenset[StudyMode] = ALL_MODES
68	
69	    def evaluate(
70	        self,
71	        context: Mapping[str, Any],
72	        *,
73	        study_modes: frozenset[StudyMode] | None = None,
74	        record: ProvenanceRecorder | None = None,
75	    ) -> GateDecision:
76	        # The gate's own `modes` is a self-declaration. When the orchestration layer supplies
77	        # the running study's actual modes, the transition must be legal under THOSE modes —
78	        # so a discovery-only run cannot pass a CONFIRM->ADJUDICATE gate even if the gate
79	        # over-declares its modes (audit pass 2, issue #23). Fall back to self.modes only
80	        # until the runner is wired.
81	        effective_modes = self.modes if study_modes is None else (study_modes & self.modes)
82	        if not legal_transition(self.frm, self.to, effective_modes):
83	            raise GateError(
84	                f"gate {self.name!r} declares an illegal transition for the effective modes: "
85	                f"{self.frm.name} -> {self.to.name}"
86	            )
87	        decision = self.precondition(context)
88	        if not decision.approved:
89	            raise GateError(f"gate {self.name!r} blocked transition: {decision.reason}")
90	        if self.requires_human and record is None:
91	            # Fail loud: a human-in-the-loop gate must not pass without a recorder to
92	            # capture the approval to the (eventual) append-only provenance trail.
93	            raise GateError(
94	                f"gate {self.name!r} requires recorded human approval but no provenance "
95	                "recorder was supplied"
96	            )
97	        if record is not None:
98	            record(decision)
99	        return decision
100

185	    gate_handler: GateHandler,
186	    tracer: Tracer | None = None,
187	    tracker: ExperimentTracker | None = None,
188	    versioner: DataVersioner | None = None,
189	    clock: Clock | None = None,
190	    secret: bytes | None = None,
191	    trail: ProvenanceTrail | None = None,
192	) -> StudyResult:
193	    """Run one study end-to-end through its governed phase sequence. See module docstring.
194	
195	    ``gate_handler`` is **required** (no default), so governance review is a conscious choice —
196	    pass :func:`auto_approve` to opt out of human review explicitly, or an operator handler that
197	    can block/park. ``tracer`` defaults to :class:`OtelTracer` (real spans if OpenTelemetry + a
198	    provider are present, a graceful no-op otherwise). ``tracker`` (optional) logs params/metrics
199	    to an experiment store, best-effort (a tracker failure never aborts the run). ``versioner``
200	    (optional) content-addresses the source so the trail cites retrievable bytes. ``clock``
201	    injects a deterministic clock for the provenance timestamps only (ordering checks always use
202	    real time). ``secret`` keys the internal provenance trail with HMAC (forgery-resistant);
203	    mutually exclusive with passing your own ``trail``. ``trail`` may be a caller-owned
204	    :class:`ProvenanceTrail` so a run that *raises* still leaves the caller the partial trail.
205	
206	    Raises ``IngestionError`` on a bad source, ``FirewallViolation``/``PreRegistrationError`` on
207	    a methodological breach, ``GateError`` if a gate blocks, and ``ValueError`` on a malformed run.
208	    """
209	    tracer = tracer or OtelTracer()
210	    if trail is not None and secret is not None:
211	        raise ValueError("pass either a caller-owned trail OR a secret to key a new one, not both")
212	    defn = plan.definition
213	    modes = defn.modes
214	    required = required_phases(modes)
215	    trail = trail if trail is not None else ProvenanceTrail(secret=secret, clock=clock)
216	    visited: list[Phase] = []
217	    run_id: str | None = None
218	
219	    def enter(phase: Phase, **payload: Any) -> None:
220	        visited.append(phase)
221	        trail.record("phase", f"enter {phase.name}", phase=phase.name, **payload)
222	
223	    def track(fn: Callable[[ExperimentTracker, str], None]) -> None:
224	        # observability is best-effort: a tracker failure is recorded but never aborts a run.
225	        if tracker is None or run_id is None:
226	            return
227	        try:
228	            fn(tracker, run_id)
229	        except Exception as exc:  # noqa: BLE001 — tracker is an external, optional backend
230	            trail.record("tracking_error", f"experiment tracker call failed: {exc}")
231	
232	    def run_gate(review: GateReview) -> None:
233	        # Record the decision once (snapshot approved/reason) and block on rejection (#94).
234	        decision = gate_handler(review)
235	        approved = bool(getattr(decision, "approved", False))
236	        reason = str(getattr(decision, "reason", ""))
237	        trail.record(
238	            "gate",
239	            f"gate {review.gate!r}: {'approved' if approved else 'blocked'}",
240	            gate=review.gate,
241	            approved=approved,
242	            reason=reason,
243	            transition=f"{review.frm.name}->{review.to.name}",
244	        )

content.text: I have a complete picture. Let me run a quick PoC to confirm the race is real in principle (the class itself has no lock), to ground the technical claim.

input.command:
cd /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay && .venv/bin/python -c "
import threading, datetime
from assay_engine.provenance import ProvenanceTrail, verify_records
import assay_engine.provenance as prov

# Wedge the digest step to widen the race window between read(seq/prev) and append.
orig = prov._digest
barrier = threading.Barrier(2)
def slow_digest(*a, **k):
    barrier.wait(timeout=5)  # both threads land here AFTER reading seq/prev_hash
    return orig(*a, **k)
prov._digest = slow_digest

t = ProvenanceTrail()
errs = []
def worker(i):
    try:
        t.record('k', f's{i}')
    except Exception as e:
        errs.append(e)
ts = [threading.Thread(target=worker, args=(i,)) for i in range(2)]
for x in ts: x.start()
for x in ts: x.join()
prov._digest = orig
print('entries:', len(t))
print('seqs:', [e.seq for e in t.entries])
try:
    verify_records(t.entries)
    print('VERIFY OK')
except Exception as e:
    print('VERIFY FAILED ->', e)
print('worker errs:', errs)
"

content.content:
entries: 2
seqs: [0, 0]
VERIFY FAILED -> provenance entry 1 has seq 0 (reordered or removed)
worker errs: []

content.text:
The PoC confirms the technical mechanism: with the read-compute-append window widened, two concurrent `record()` calls both read `seq=0`/`prev_hash=genesis`, append two entries both claiming `seq=0`, and `verify_records` fails — a corrupted chain. The race is real at the class level.

Now the production-readiness assessment. The exposure analysis in the finding is accurate and I've confirmed every claim:

1. `run_study` (pipeline.py) is strictly single-threaded — every `trail.record` is an inline call within sequential phase spans. No concurrency.
2. The one `ThreadPoolExecutor` (reasoning/seam.py:136) runs only backend-call closures via `_submit_bounded`; it never touches the trail (grep for trail/provenance in reasoning/ returns nothing relevant).
3. `as_recorder()` is defined and tested but never wired into the engine — gates receive a `record` callback, but in pipeline.py the gate path uses the inline `run_gate`/`trail.record`, not `as_recorder`, and still single-threaded.
4. The class docstring scopes the trail to "one study run" and the `__init__` defaults make a fresh trail per run.

So there is no live concurrent caller. The defect is latent. Notably, the codebase elsewhere demonstrates explicit thread-safety where it matters (registry.py `_LOCK`, tracing.py `_provider_lock`, checkpoint.py `_init_lock`, seam.py `_inflight_lock`), which shows the team locks shared mutable state when concurrency is real — and conversely, the trail is deliberately not locked because it isn't shared.

This is a real latent defect (public `record()`/`as_recorder()` API with mutable shared state, no documented single-thread constraint, no assertion), but with zero current exposure. The original LOW severity is appropriate, arguably bordering on a hardening nice-to-have. It is a genuine production-readiness item (a future wiring would silently corrupt the audit trail that is the system's integrity spine), not a false positive.

input.reasoning:
CONFIRMED as a real but latent production-readiness defect; original LOW severity is correct.

Technical claim verified by reading src/assay_engine/provenance.py:112-150. record() reads shared mutable state without a lock — seq=len(self._entries) (line 116) and prev_hash=self._entries[-1].entry_hash (line 117) — then does clock/normalize/_digest work (lines 118-134), then appends (line 149). The read-compute-append is a non-atomic check-then-act on self._entries. I reproduced the corruption with a PoC against the venv: by wedging _digest behind a 2-thread Barrier (widening the window between the seq/prev read and the append), two concurrent record() calls both read seq=0/prev_hash=genesis and append two entries both claiming seq=0, and verify_records() then raised 'provenance entry 1 has seq 0 (reordered or removed)'. So a shared trail under concurrency would silently corrupt the append-only chain — the exact 'no step removed or reordered' guarantee the trail exists to provide (GOVERNANCE §3, module docstring).

Exposure analysis in the finding is accurate; I independently confirmed every part: (1) run_study in pipeline.py is strictly single-threaded — all ~15 trail.record calls are inline within sequential phase spans (enter(), run_gate(), track() all call record() synchronously, lines 221/230/237/250/284/322/342/365/386/417/458/465/488/509). (2) The only ThreadPoolExecutor (reasoning/seam.py:136) executes only backend-call closures via _submit_bounded/_with_timeout; grep for trail|provenance|recorder in src/assay_engine/reasoning/ returns nothing that touches the trail. (3) as_recorder() (provenance.py:152) is defined and tested but NOT wired anywhere in the engine — gates.py:74-98 accept a ProvenanceRecorder, but pipeline.py uses inline run_gate/trail.record, never as_recorder, and still single-threaded. (4) A fresh ProvenanceTrail is created per run (pipeline.py:215) and the docstring scopes it to 'one study run.' So no concurrent caller exists today; the defect is latent.

Not a false positive: it is a genuine class-level defect (public record()/as_recorder() API over shared mutable state, no lock, no documented single-thread constraint, no assertion). It is not excluded by any hard constraint. The team locks shared state elsewhere when concurrency is real (registry.py _LOCK, tracing.py _provider_lock, checkpoint.py _init_lock, seam.py _inflight_lock), so a lock here (or an explicit single-thread assertion/doc) is consistent with the codebase's own standard. Severity LOW is correct: zero live exposure today, but a future concurrent gate/recorder wiring would silently corrupt the integrity spine, and there is nothing in the API to prevent that.
input.evidence: Read src/assay_engine/provenance.py (full): record() lines 116-117 read self._entries (seq, prev_hash), line 149 appends — no lock; verify_records() lines 224-226 assert e.seq==i and e.prev_hash==prev. PoC against /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/.venv/bin/python: monkeypatched _digest behind a threading.Barrier(2), ran 2 threads calling record() on one shared trail; result was 2 entries with seqs [0,0] and verify_records raised 'provenance entry 1 has seq 0 (reordered or removed)' — concrete chain corruption. Exposure: grep across src/ for ThreadPool/threading/concurrent shows the only pool is reasoning/seam.py:136 (backend-call closures only; no trail access — confirmed by grep trail|provenance|recorder in src/assay_engine/reasoning/ = no hits). grep for as_recorder|ProvenanceRecorder|recorder= shows as_recorder defined at provenance.py:152, typed in gates.py:34/74, used only in tests/test_provenance.py:140-156 — never wired in engine code. pipeline.py:215 creates a per-run trail; all record() calls (enter/run_gate/track and inline) are synchronous and sequential. Comparable locks elsewhere: registry.py:22 _LOCK, observability/tracing.py:35 _provider_lock, persistence/checkpoint.py:59 _init_lock, reasoning/seam.py:138 _inflight_lock.

content.text:
Verified. The finding is a TRUE production-readiness defect, correctly rated LOW.

Summary: `ProvenanceTrail.record()` at /Users/[REDACTED]/hc-assay/src/assay_engine/provenance.py:112-150 performs a non-atomic read-compute-append on the shared `self._entries` list (read seq/prev_hash at lines 116-117, append at line 149) with no lock. I reproduced the corruption with a venv PoC — two concurrent record() calls produced two entries both claiming seq=0, and verify_records() failed. The exposure analysis is accurate: no live concurrent caller exists (run_study is sequential, the reasoning ThreadPool never touches the trail, as_recorder is defined/tested but unwired), so it is latent. LOW is the right severity; the codebase locks shared state elsewhere (registry, tracing, checkpoint, seam), so a lock or explicit single-thread contract here would match its own standard.
